data.output:
/tmp/story-4-final.md
# Story 4 Final Plan — Document viewer should focus on `docs/`, not `.agents/.claude` mirrors

## Diagnosis (unchanged from draft)

`Query.documents` (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_documents.go`) calls
`docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig`, which falls back to `BuildGraph(workingDir)`
when no `.ddx/graphs/*.yaml` is present (this repo's case). `BuildGraph` walks
the project root via `findMarkdownFiles`
(`cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:439`). The hard-coded skip-list at line
461 is `.git, .ddx, .claude, worktrees`. `.agents` is missing.

The repo carries a full `docs/helix/` mirror under
`.agents/skills/docs/helix/`. Every artifact ID therefore appears at least
twice (canonical + `.agents` mirror), accounting for ~all of the 87
`duplicate_id` audit issues in this repo. Detail views resolve to whichever
file `path_to_id` wrote last, which is why the user sees non-canonical
content in the web UI.

`docs/resources/` and `docs/exec/` migration leftovers contribute a small
additional set, already owned by `ddx-58764e1b` prong 1.

## Relationship to `ddx-58764e1b`

**Recommendation: fold Story 4 into `ddx-58764e1b`.** Codex concurred. That
bead is already open, names `.agents/.claude` scan overlap as the suspected
root cause for prong 2, and has a zero-duplicates AC that requires Story 4's
fix to satisfy. Splitting risks two agents racing on the same scanner code
and on overlapping audit-snapshot evidence. Update the bead's description
to confirm root cause and add explicit ACs for the web viewer and the
FEAT-005 amendment.

## Architectural choice

**Scanner-layer fix (option A), not viewer-layer (option B).** Codex agreed.
The viewer is a passive consumer of the graph; filtering there leaves
`docGraph`, audit, and future consumers polluted with phantom duplicates.

Codex flagged one important wrinkle: if a project explicitly configures
`.ddx/graphs/*.yaml roots:` pointing into `.agents/`, should the hard-coded
skip-list still apply? Two acceptable answers:

- (i) **Skip-list always wins.** Simplest, matches today's behaviour for
  `.claude` (no escape hatch exists either). Recommended.
- (ii) **Explicit root overrides skip.** More flexible, but adds a code
  path with no current consumer.

Plan: take (i), add a test that documents the precedence so future readers
don't have to guess.

## FEAT-005 amendment (per codex)

Codex pushed back on framing the change as a pure clarification. The current
prose ("DDx discovers artifacts by scanning … not by looking in specific
directories") is misleading. Amendment: change the discovery section to say
discovery is **content/frontmatter-based within configured scan roots,
subject to an exclusion list of tool-managed and storage directories
(`.git, .ddx, .claude, .agents, worktrees`).** Rationale paragraph names the
exclusion category ("tool-managed mirrors, agent scratch space, VCS
internals") so future agent harnesses (`.crush`, `.pi`, etc.) have a
principled home.

## Implementation surface

1. `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go:461` — add `".agents"` to the switch.
   Update the comment to explain the exclusion category (tool-managed agent
   workspaces; mirrors of canonical `docs/`).
2. `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` — three new tests:
   - `TestFindMarkdownFiles_SkipsAgentsDir` — `docs/foo.md` and
     `.agents/skills/docs/foo.md` (same ID); graph contains only the
     canonical entry.
   - `TestFindMarkdownFiles_SkipsAgentsSidecar` — same as above but the
     duplicate is conveyed via `foo.md.ddx.yaml` sidecar; graph still
     contains only the canonical entry. (Codex add.)
   - `TestFindMarkdownFiles_SkipListWinsOverExplicitRoots` — a graph config
     `roots: [".agents/skills/docs"]` still produces no entries from
     `.agents/`. Documents precedence. (Codex add.)
3. `cli/internal/server/graphql_documents_test.go` — add a smoke that mirrors
   a doc into `.agents/skills/docs/...` and asserts `Query.documents` returns
   one entry with path under `docs/`. (Secondary; docgraph tests are the
   primary pin.)
4. `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-005-artifacts.md` — substantive
   amendment to the discovery section per the FEAT-005 paragraph above; not
   a one-liner.
5. `ddx-58764e1b` — update description; merge Story 4 into it; refine ACs to
   include web viewer and FEAT-005 amendment.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `ddx doc audit` reports zero `duplicate_id` issues attributable to
   `.agents/` paths.
2. After also resolving `docs/resources/` leftovers, `ddx doc audit` reports
   zero `duplicate_id` issues total.
3. Web UI documents list shows each canonical ID exactly once; detail view
   loads the file under `docs/`.
4. New unit tests in `docgraph_test.go` pass (skip dir, skip sidecar,
   skip-wins-over-roots).
5. FEAT-005 documents the exclusion list and the rationale, replacing the
   misleading "directory-agnostic" framing.
6. Audit before/after evidence captured under `.ddx/executions/<run>/`.

## Risks

- **Skill distribution coupling.** `.agents/skills/` is part of DDx's skill
  packaging mechanism. Excluding it from the project docgraph is correct,
  but verify no command (e.g., `ddx doc validate`, persona/skill loaders)
  relies on docgraph scanning distributed skill docs as project artifacts.
  Quick grep confirms skills are loaded by their own loader, not docgraph,
  but the bead should re-verify before commit.
- **Basename match at any depth.** `some/path/.agents/docs` would also be
  skipped. Acceptable; document in FEAT-005.
- **Whack-a-mole on future agent dirs** (`.crush`, `.pi`, nested `.agents`).
  Codex flagged. Mitigation: don't try to fix in this bead, but the
  FEAT-005 amendment names the *category* so the next addition is a
  principled extension, not an ad-hoc skip.
- **Legitimate non-`docs/` artifacts.** None today. New tests pin
  behaviour so any future regression is loud.

## Bead breakdown

**Single bead — extend `ddx-58764e1b`.** Updated scope:

- Title (unchanged): "Resolve 90 duplicate-ID issues reported by ddx doc
  audit"
- Description additions:
  - Confirm root cause for prong 2: `.agents/` is not in the docgraph
    skip-list.
  - Fix: add `.agents` to skip-list in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go`,
    update comment, add tests.
  - Amend FEAT-005 to define the exclusion category.
- New ACs (additive to existing four):
  - Web UI documents list/detail load canonical `docs/` files.
  - FEAT-005 names the exclusion list and category rationale.
  - Three new docgraph unit tests pass.
- Tier: **low-to-medium**. Code change is small and deterministic; the
  FEAT-005 amendment requires a few sentences of careful prose. Cheap
  model can do the code; review pass for the FEAT-005 wording.

**Optional follow-on (do not file unless requested):**

- Configurable exclude list in `.ddx/graphs/*.yaml` (`excludes: [...]`),
  for projects that want to deviate from the hard-coded default. Tier:
  medium. Defer until a second concrete need appears.

## Open questions for user

1. Confirm: fold Story 4 into `ddx-58764e1b` (recommended) vs file as new
   sibling bead?
2. Confirm precedence rule: hard-coded skip-list always wins over explicit
   `.ddx/graphs/*.yaml roots:` (recommended) vs explicit roots override
   default skip?
3. Should the FEAT-005 amendment be done in this same bead (recommended)
   or split as a docs-only follow-on?

data.text: The epic's AC requires all child beads closed. Child `ddx-2502ad71` is still open, so the epic can't close yet.

input.content:
Bead ddx-781af038 is an epic whose acceptance is "All child beads closed."

Children (label story:4, parent ddx-781af038):
- ddx-2502ad71 — open — "scanner: extend skip-list to .ddx/plugins/* + complete duplicate-ID cleanup"

Status:
- One child remains open. The epic cannot legitimately close until ddx-2502ad71 closes.
- No epic-level work is appropriate in this attempt; the contract is purely aggregation over children.

What a follow-up needs:
- Execute ddx-2502ad71 (scanner skip-list extension to .ddx/plugins/*, FEAT-005 prose amendment, audit before/after evidence, new docgraph test). Once that closes, this epic can be closed by the orchestrator or by a trivial follow-up attempt.

data.text: No code changes. The epic ddx-781af038 aggregates children; child ddx-2502ad71 is still open, so the epic's AC ("all child beads closed") cannot be satisfied in this attempt. Rationale written to `.ddx/executions/20260502T115139-210a06e8/no_changes_rationale.txt`.
